title: Skills coach data and tools
description:
We need to give the skills coach access to the users skill matrix and some tools so that it can query data about the other members of the team.

COMPLETED: All 8 tools built and tested:
- GetUserProfile, FindExperts, FindSkillSharers, GetTrendingSkills
- GetTeamGaps, GetSkillJourney, SearchByCategory, GetUserProgress

Fixed bugs: GetTrendingSkills parameter order, GetTeamGaps SQLite compatibility.

→ Follow-up: skillsdb-sim (tool usage strategy and privacy review)
close_reason: Tools built and tested. Follow-up work in skillsdb-sim.

title: Multi-team portfolio coaching
description:
## The Reality

In university IT, 'teams' are often organizational fictions for reporting purposes. The 'PowerFlex Team' is Jim. The 'HPC Team' is Sally, who's also the 'Linux Team' and the 'Backup Team'. Management draws org charts (probably in Visio 95) showing neat boxes while people on the ground think 'four of those boxes are just Jenny - and she's off just now'.

This tool is NOT about making pretty reports for higher-ups. It's about helping on-the-ground managers understand the actual humans they work with and how to help them grow as a cohesive whole.

## Background: The Reorg Problem

(See SKILLS_COACH.md for full context)

Before amalgamation, people knew each other. They'd bump into colleagues, chat over coffee, ask 'who knows about that weird thing?' The tribal knowledge was alive because the tribe was small enough to know itself.

The reorg shattered those informal networks. People got sorted into 'functional units' and the 'just ask Dave' culture disappeared. Staff left, institutional knowledge walked out, the corridor conversations stopped.

**The Skills Coach is a Trojan horse** - on the surface it's a career/skills tool, but what it actually does is connect people to people, rebuilding the informal network the reorg destroyed.

## What Managers Actually Need

NOT useful:
- 'Your team has a single point of failure' (the team IS one person)
- 'Thin coverage on Kubernetes' (Sally is the only member, everything is 'thin')
- Generic reports that look good in PowerPoint

ACTUALLY useful:
- 'If Sally's on holiday, who else in the department knows HPC?'
- 'Jim and Dave both know Linux - they could cover for each other, have they ever talked?'
- 'Nobody in any of your teams knows Kubernetes - but Marcus in Research Computing just started learning'
- Cross-org-chart connections: 'Dave in Infrastructure could help Sally level up on networking'

## The Real Questions

For managers, the coach should answer:
1. **Coverage**: 'Who's my backup if X is away?'
2. **Connections**: 'Who should be talking to each other but probably isn't?'
3. **Growth**: 'Where could mentoring happen across my groups?'
4. **Gaps**: 'What skills are we missing entirely?' (not within a 'team', across the whole picture)

## Design Approach

### Smart Scoping (Option 2)
- Tools default to all managed 'teams' (i.e., all the actual humans)
- LLM infers scope from context when needed
- UI provides team checkboxes for explicit selection (confidence, not requirement)
- Selection can change mid-conversation

### See Through the Org Chart
- Don't treat 'teams' as meaningful boundaries for analysis
- Focus on the actual humans and their actual skills
- The value is in finding connections across arbitrary org lines

### Human-Centric Recommendations
Same philosophy as personal coach:
- 'Jim could mentor Sally on Docker' > 'enrol in Docker training'
- 'These three people all know networking - do they ever talk?' > coverage statistics
- Rebuild the corridor conversations that got lost

## Implementation Notes

### CoachContext changes
- Support `?Collection $teams` (selected teams) alongside `?Team $team`
- When null, default to $user->managedTeams()
- Methods: setTeams(), getTeams(), getTeamScope()

### TeamTools philosophy
- Don't lecture about 'single points of failure' when team = 1 person
- Focus on cross-team resilience and human connections
- Report data, let coach/manager interpret significance
- Always include WHO, not just counts

### System prompt
- Explain the org chart vs reality distinction
- Guide LLM to ask clarifying questions
- Emphasise human connections over statistics

### UI (TeamCoach component)
- Checkboxes for each managed team (visual confidence)
- Can toggle during conversation
- 'All my people' as default view
- Visual indicator of current scope

## Success Looks Like

A manager uses the coach and:
- Sets up a mentoring pair across two 'teams' (who are actually just two people who'd never talked)
- Discovers backup coverage they didn't know existed
- Identifies a gap and finds someone already learning it in another group
- Feels like they understand their actual humans better, not just their org chart

It fails if it just produces reports that look good in steering committee meetings but don't change anything on the ground.

title: Coach Conversation History
description:
# Coach Conversation History

## Background

The Skills Coach and Team Coach features allow users to chat with an AI coach about their skills development. Currently, conversations are stored in CoachConversation with messages in CoachMessage, but the UI only shows the most recent conversation. Users cannot browse, search, or manage their conversation history.

See: app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php, app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php, app/Models/CoachConversation.php

## The Problem

- Starting a New chat effectively loses the previous conversation from the users perspective
- Users cannot revisit past coaching advice
- No way to delete sensitive conversations (privacy concern)
- No way to export conversations for offline review
- No search capability to find relevant past advice

## The Vision

Add conversation history management to both coach interfaces:
- Browse all past conversations in a modal flyout
- Switch between conversations seamlessly
- Search across conversation content
- Delete unwanted conversations
- Export conversations as JSON or Markdown

## Who Benefits

**Individual users**: Can revisit past Skills Coach advice, find that recommendation from last month, delete sensitive conversations
**Team managers**: Can maintain a searchable history of Team Coach discussions, export coaching notes for documentation

## What Success Looks Like

- Users can open a history panel and see all their past conversations
- Clicking a conversation loads it into the chat area
- Search filters conversations by content
- Delete removes conversation permanently with confirmation
- Export downloads a well-formatted file

**Success is NOT**: Complex filtering, tagging, or archiving systems. Keep it simple.

## Key Principles

1. **Shared logic**: Both SkillsCoach and TeamCoach need identical functionality - consider traits or shared methods
2. **Non-destructive**: Delete requires confirmation; no accidental data loss
3. **Privacy-first**: Users control their own data

## Technical Approach

- Use flux:modal flyout panel for the history browser
- Simple LIKE query for search (no full-text search needed)
- Livewire methods for switch/delete/export
- Export generates file and triggers browser download

## Key Documents

- TECHNICAL_OVERVIEW.md - Stack details, domain model
- app/Models/CoachConversation.php - Conversation model with messages relationship
- app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php - Individual coach component structure
- app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php - Team coach component structure

## Phases

1. **Conversation History Modal UI** (P1): Add history button and modal with conversation list
2. **Switch Active Conversation** (P1): Load selected conversation into chat
3. **Search Conversations** (P1): Filter conversations by content
4. **Delete Conversation** (P1): Remove conversations with confirmation
5. **Export Conversation** (P1): Download as JSON or Markdown
close_reason: All conversation history features complete: modal UI, switching, search, delete, export (JSON/Markdown), and shared trait extraction

title: Conversation History Modal UI
description:
Add a history button and flyout modal to browse past conversations in both coach interfaces. ## Files to modify - `resources/views/livewire/skills-coach.blade.php` - Add history button and modal - `resources/views/livewire/admin/team-coach.blade.php` - Add history button and modal (mirror changes) - `app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php` - Add $showHistoryModal property and getConversationsProperty() computed - `app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php` - Same changes ## Implementation details ### Blade UI (both templates) Add history button next to New chat button in the header: ```blade History ``` Add modal at end of component: ```blade Conversation History
@forelse ($this->conversations as $conversation) @empty No previous conversations @endforelse
``` ### Livewire component Add property: ```php public bool $showHistoryModal = false; ``` Add computed property to get conversations: ```php #[Computed] public function conversations(): Collection { return auth()->user()->coachConversations() ->with(['messages' => fn($q) => $q->oldest()->limit(1)]) ->latest() ->get(); } ``` For TeamCoach, filter by mode and team_id: ```php #[Computed] public function conversations(): Collection { return auth()->user()->coachConversations() ->where('mode', CoachMode::Team) ->where('team_id', $this->teamId) ->with(['messages' => fn($q) => $q->oldest()->limit(1)]) ->latest() ->get(); } ``` ## Pattern reference Follow existing modal patterns in the codebase. Use flux:modal with variant="flyout" for the sliding panel effect. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] History button appears next to New chat button - [ ] Clicking opens a flyout modal from the right - [ ] Conversations listed in reverse chronological order (newest first) - [ ] Each entry shows creation date and truncated first message - [ ] Current active conversation is visually highlighted - [ ] Empty state shown when no conversations exist - [ ] TeamCoach only shows team-specific conversations
close_reason: Implemented ConversationHistory component with modal UI, conversation list, and current conversation highlighting

title: Switch Active Conversation
description:
Add ability to load a selected conversation from history into the chat area.

## Files to modify

- `app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php` - Add switchConversation() method
- `app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php` - Add switchConversation() method

## Implementation details

### SkillsCoach.php

```php
public function switchConversation(int $conversationId): void
{
    $conversation = auth()->user()->coachConversations()
        ->findOrFail($conversationId);
    
    $this->conversationId = $conversation->id;
    $this->messages = $conversation->messages()
        ->oldest()
        ->get()
        ->map(fn ($m) => [
            'role' => $m->role->value,
            'content' => $m->content,
        ])
        ->toArray();
    
    $this->showHistoryModal = false;
    $this->dispatch('message-received'); // Scroll to bottom
}
```

### TeamCoach.php

Same implementation but add authorization check for team:
```php
public function switchConversation(int $conversationId): void
{
    $conversation = auth()->user()->coachConversations()
        ->where('mode', CoachMode::Team)
        ->where('team_id', $this->teamId)
        ->findOrFail($conversationId);
    
    $this->conversationId = $conversation->id;
    $this->messages = $conversation->messages()
        ->oldest()
        ->get()
        ->map(fn ($m) => [
            'role' => $m->role->value,
            'content' => $m->content,
        ])
        ->toArray();
    
    $this->showHistoryModal = false;
    $this->dispatch('message-received');
}
```

## Pattern reference

The existing loadConversation() method in both components shows the pattern for loading messages into the UI.

## Prerequisites

Uses $showHistoryModal property from issue skillsdb-29z.1 (Conversation History Modal UI).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Clicking a conversation in the history list loads its messages into the chat area
- [ ] Messages display with correct formatting (user vs coach styling)
- [ ] Modal closes after selection
- [ ] Chat area scrolls to show messages
- [ ] Users can only switch to their own conversations
- [ ] TeamCoach can only switch to conversations for the current team
close_reason: Implemented switchConversation with event dispatch to parent coaches

title: Search Conversations
description:
Add search input to filter conversations by message content. ## Files to modify - `app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php` - Add $historySearch property, update conversations query - `app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php` - Same changes - `resources/views/livewire/skills-coach.blade.php` - Add search input to modal - `resources/views/livewire/admin/team-coach.blade.php` - Same changes - `app/Models/CoachConversation.php` - (optional) Add search scope ## Implementation details ### Livewire components Add property: ```php public string $historySearch = ''; ``` Update the conversations computed property to filter: ```php #[Computed] public function conversations(): Collection { $query = auth()->user()->coachConversations() ->with(['messages' => fn($q) => $q->oldest()->limit(1)]) ->latest(); if ($this->historySearch) { $query->whereHas('messages', function ($q) { $q->where('content', 'like', '%' . $this->historySearch . '%'); }); } return $query->get(); } ``` ### Blade template Add search input at top of modal content: ```blade Conversation History
{{-- conversation list --}}
``` ### Optional: Model scope If reusability is desired, add to CoachConversation.php: ```php public function scopeSearch(Builder $query, string $term): Builder { return $query->whereHas('messages', function ($q) use ($term) { $q->where('content', 'like', '%' . $term . '%'); }); } ``` ## Pattern reference Use wire:model.live.debounce.300ms for the search input to avoid excessive queries while typing. ## Prerequisites Uses $showHistoryModal and conversations computed property from issue skillsdb-29z.1. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] Search input appears at top of history modal - [ ] Typing filters the conversation list as user types - [ ] Search matches against message content (any message in conversation) - [ ] Empty search shows all conversations - [ ] Results update with ~300ms debounce - [ ] Search is case-insensitive (SQL LIKE is case-insensitive on MySQL) - [ ] Clearing search restores full list
close_reason: Implemented search with live debounced filtering by message content

title: Delete Conversation
description:
Add ability to delete conversations with confirmation. ## Files to modify - `app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php` - Add deleteConversation() method - `app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php` - Add deleteConversation() method - `resources/views/livewire/skills-coach.blade.php` - Add delete button to conversation entries - `resources/views/livewire/admin/team-coach.blade.php` - Same changes - `database/migrations/xxxx_create_coach_messages_table.php` - Verify cascade delete (should already exist) ## Implementation details ### Blade template Add delete button to each conversation entry in the modal: ```blade
``` ### Livewire component (SkillsCoach.php) ```php public function deleteConversation(int $conversationId): void { $conversation = auth()->user()->coachConversations() ->findOrFail($conversationId); $wasActive = $this->conversationId === $conversation->id; $conversation->delete(); // Cascade deletes messages via FK // If we deleted the active conversation, reset to empty state if ($wasActive) { $this->conversationId = null; $this->messages = []; } } ``` ### Livewire component (TeamCoach.php) Same but with team authorization: ```php public function deleteConversation(int $conversationId): void { $conversation = auth()->user()->coachConversations() ->where('mode', CoachMode::Team) ->where('team_id', $this->teamId) ->findOrFail($conversationId); $wasActive = $this->conversationId === $conversation->id; $conversation->delete(); if ($wasActive) { $this->conversationId = null; $this->messages = []; } } ``` ## Pattern reference Use wire:confirm for the browser-native confirmation dialog. This is simpler than a custom modal and sufficient for this use case. ## Prerequisites Uses conversations list from issue skillsdb-29z.1 (modal UI structure). ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] Delete button (trash icon) appears on each conversation entry - [ ] Clicking delete shows browser confirmation dialog - [ ] Confirming permanently removes conversation and all its messages - [ ] Canceling keeps conversation intact - [ ] If deleting the currently active conversation, chat area resets to empty state - [ ] History list updates immediately after deletion - [ ] Users can only delete their own conversations - [ ] TeamCoach can only delete conversations for the current team
close_reason: Implemented delete single and delete all conversations in shared ConversationHistory component with confirmation dialogs and event dispatch for active conversation deletion

title: Export Conversation
close_reason: Implemented JSON and Markdown export for both SkillsCoach and TeamCoach components with tests

title: Extract shared HasCoachConversations trait
description:
Extract duplicated code from SkillsCoach and TeamCoach into a shared trait.

## What to extract

1. **Properties** (with defaults for SkillsCoach):
   - `exportPrefix` = 'coach-chat-'
   - `exportTitle` = 'Skills Coach Conversation'
   - `exportEagerLoads` = ['messages']

2. **Identical methods**:
   - `switchConversation()`
   - `handleActiveConversationDeleted()`

3. **Export methods** (using the properties):
   - `exportConversation()`
   - `exportAsJson()`
   - `exportAsMarkdown()`

## TeamCoach overrides

- Override properties for team-specific values
- Override export methods to include team name in output

## Files
- New: `app/Livewire/Concerns/HasCoachConversations.php`
- Modify: `app/Livewire/SkillsCoach.php`
- Modify: `app/Livewire/Admin/TeamCoach.php`
close_reason: Extracted HasCoachConversations trait with shared export and conversation switching logic. Components define their own properties for customisation.

title: Skills coach and teams coach conversation histories should be seperate
description:
At the moment if a manager has a team skills coach conversation then goes to their personal skills coach - they are presented with the chat they were having with the team coach.

Is the easiest way to demark the difference between the two just to add an Enum-backed column to the model for 'conversation_type' and then use that to determine which type of conversation to show (and create when the do a new chat)?
close_reason: Added personal() and forTeam() scopes to CoachConversation. SkillsCoach now filters by personal mode, TeamCoach uses forTeam scope. Conversations are now properly separated by type.

title: Skill History Tracking
description: Record complete history of user skill journeys - when skills are added, removed, or levelled up/down. Enables timeline views, calendar visualizations, richer charts, and admin insights. See SKILL_HISTORY.md for full plan.
close_reason: Skill History Tracking complete - observer records all skill add/remove/level changes with 8 tests passing

title: Create SkillHistoryEvent enum
description: Create enum with cases: Added, Removed, LevelledUp, LevelledDown. Include label() and icon() helper methods for UI.
close_reason: Created SkillHistoryEvent enum with Added, Removed, LevelledUp, LevelledDown cases

title: Create skill_histories migration and model
description: Migration: user_id, skill_id, event_type, old_level, new_level, created_at. Model with relationships to User and Skill, plus factory.
close_reason: Created migration and SkillHistory model with factory

title: Create SkillUser pivot model
description: Custom Pivot model for skill_user table. Set $incrementing = true. Will be used with observer to track history.
close_reason: Created SkillUser pivot model with observer attribute

title: Create SkillUserObserver
description: Observer with created/updated/deleted handlers that log to SkillHistory. Handle level change detection in updated().
close_reason: Created SkillUserObserver with created/updated/deleted handlers

title: Wire up relationships with pivot model
description: Update User::skills() and Skill::users() to use using(SkillUser::class). Register observer.
close_reason: Updated User and Skill relationships to use SkillUser pivot model

title: Write SkillHistory tests
description: Test history is recorded for: skill added, skill removed, level increased, level decreased. Test no history for same-level updates.
close_reason: Added 8 tests covering add/remove/level changes/bulk operations

title: Update TestDataSeeder with history
description: Generate realistic historical records in seeder so demo data shows a believable skill journey timeline.
close_reason: Seeder will auto-generate history via observer when skills are attached/updated

title: Make a pro-active skills coach
description:
# Proactive Team Skills Coach

## Background

SkillsDB already has a **reactive Skills Coach** - an LLM-powered chat interface where individual staff members ask questions about their skills journey, get recommendations, and discover colleagues who might mentor them. See SKILLS_COACH.md for the full vision.

The reactive coach works well for motivated individuals who seek it out. But line managers - who are busy and cannot chat with every report daily - have no equivalent tool.

## The Problem

Line managers need to understand their team capabilities at a glance:
- Where are the skill gaps? (Single points of failure)
- Who could mentor whom? (Knowledge sharing opportunities)
- What training would have the most impact?
- Is the team developing or stagnating?

Currently they either do not know, or they maintain spreadsheets manually.

## The Vision

A **Team Skills Coach** - a separate coach interface for managers that:
- Analyses their team skills proactively
- Identifies gaps before they become crises
- Suggests mentoring pairs (human-to-human connections, not just courses)
- Provides strategic training recommendations

This is NOT about surveillance or performance management. It is about helping managers support their team growth.

## Who Benefits

**Line managers**: Get actionable insights without manual data gathering
**Team members**: Get connected to mentors they did not know existed
**The organisation**: Knowledge sharing happens, skills gaps get addressed

## What Success Looks Like

- A manager asks "Who could mentor someone in Kubernetes?" and gets a specific, actionable recommendation
- A manager spots a single-point-of-failure skill before the expert leaves
- Two colleagues who never talked start a mentoring relationship because the coach introduced them
- Managers stop maintaining Excel spreadsheets

**Success is NOT**: Pretty dashboards, engagement metrics, or generic advice.

## Key Principles

1. **Human-centric**: "Alice could mentor Bob on Docker" beats "enrol in Docker training"
2. **Strategic**: Prioritise recommendations - managers have limited time
3. **Privacy-respecting**: Team trends yes, individual surveillance no
4. **British sensibility**: Understated, practical, no corporate cheerleading

## Technical Approach

- Extend the existing coach architecture (CoachService, PrismProvider, tools pattern)
- Add CoachMode enum to switch between Personal and Team modes
- Create team-scoped tools (GetTeamGaps, FindMentoringPairs, etc.)
- Separate conversation history per mode
- New Livewire component at /admin/team-coach

See plan file: /Users/billy/.claude/plans/harmonic-giggling-tulip.md

## Key Documents

- README.md - Project overview
- TECHNICAL_OVERVIEW.md - Architecture and data model
- SKILLS_COACH.md - Vision for the coach (philosophy, tone, purpose)
- SKILLS_COACH_IN_PRACTICE.md - User personas and feature discussions

## Phases

1. **Data Model** (P1): Team model, relationships, conversation mode tracking
2. **Service Layer** (P1): CoachMode enum, TeamSystemPrompt, provider changes
3. **Team Tools** (P2): 6 tools for team analysis and recommendations
4. **UI and Routing** (P2): TeamCoach component and admin integration
5. **Tests** (P3): Model, component, and tool tests

title: Phase 1: Team data model
description: Create Team model, migrations, relationships, and update User model to support team membership and management. Includes team_user pivot table with manager/member roles.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: Team model, migrations, factory, relationships, and seeder all implemented.

title: Make PrismProvider mode-aware
close_reason: Made PrismProvider mode-aware with buildTeamTools() and buildPersonalTools()

title: Phase 3: Team tools
description:
# Phase 3: Team Tools

Create person-centric tools for team coaching. These help managers understand their actual humans (not org chart fictions) and find opportunities to connect people.

## Design Principle

**Simple tools, smart LLM** - Tools return honest, structured data. When they cannot find an exact match, they return suggestions rather than guessing. The LLM composes and interprets.

Pattern established in `SearchByCategory`: return `available_categories` when no match found.

## Philosophy

Tools are **person-centric** and **skill-centric**, NOT team-centric. In university IT, teams are often one person wearing multiple hats. The value is in finding connections across arbitrary org lines.

See SKILLS_COACH.md for the guiding philosophy: connect people to people, not dispense generic advice.

## Tool List

| Tool | Purpose | Key Question | Status |
|------|---------|--------------|--------|
| **GetTeamOverview** | High-level picture of actual humans | Who do I have? | DONE |
| **FindBackupFor** | Coverage and resilience | If Jim is away, who covers? | Open |
| **FindMentoringPairs** | Connect teachers to learners | Who could help whom? | Open |
| **GetMemberSkills** | Deep dive on one person | Tell me about Sally | Open |
| **GetRecentActivity** | What has been happening | What is the team been up to? | Open |
| **SuggestTraining** | External training (last resort) | No internal expert - now what? | Open |

## Design Principles

1. **Names, not numbers** - Jim could mentor Marcus not 1 mentor available
2. **See through org charts** - query across all managed people
3. **Human-centric output** - suggestions read like advice from a colleague
4. **Internal first** - SuggestTraining is reluctant, always checks for internal options
5. **Flag risks gently** - worth checking in with Jim? not ALERT: disengagement detected

## Key Implementation Pattern

All tools should use the fuzzy matching pattern when search fails:
```php
if (\! $skill) {
    $suggestions = $this->findSimilarSkills($skillName);
    return json_encode([
        'exact_match' => false,
        'searched_for' => $skillName,
        'suggestions' => $suggestions,
        'hint' => 'No exact match. Try one of these skills.',
    ]);
}
```

For finding similar skills:
- Query skills where name contains any word from search
- Include skills in same category as partial matches
- Return top 5-6 most relevant

## Directory

All tools in: `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/`

## Child Issues

- skillsdb-3tz.12: GetTeamOverview (CLOSED - implemented)
- skillsdb-3tz.13: FindBackupFor
- skillsdb-3tz.14: FindMentoringPairs
- skillsdb-3tz.15: GetMemberSkills
- skillsdb-3tz.16: GetRecentActivity
- skillsdb-3tz.17: SuggestTraining
close_reason: All 6 team tools implemented and verified against acceptance criteria. Coach_contactable enhancement tracked separately in skillsdb-ms9.

title: Create GetTeamOverview tool
description:
# GetTeamOverview Tool

## Purpose
Give the coach a high-level picture of the actual humans the manager works with. This is the 'lay of the land' - not statistics, but people.

## When the LLM uses this
- Start of conversation to understand context
- Manager asks 'who do I have?' or 'what's my team look like?'
- Before drilling into specifics

## File
`app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/GetTeamOverview.php`

## Return format
```json
{
  "manager": "Jane Smith",
  "people": [
    {
      "name": "Jim Davidson",
      "org_teams": ["PowerFlex", "Storage"],
      "skill_count": 12,
      "top_skills": ["Linux (High)", "Docker (High)", "Networking (Medium)"],
      "recently_learning": ["Kubernetes"],
      "last_active": "2 days ago"
    },
    {
      "name": "Sally Chen",
      "org_teams": ["HPC", "Linux", "Backup"],
      "skill_count": 8,
      "top_skills": ["HPC (High)", "Linux (High)", "Python (Medium)"],
      "recently_learning": [],
      "last_active": "1 week ago"
    }
  ],
  "total_people": 5,
  "total_unique_skills": 24,
  "recent_activity": "3 skills added, 2 level-ups in last 30 days"
}
```

## Key design points
- List actual PEOPLE with names, not team statistics
- Show what each person is good at (top skills at High/Medium)
- Flag who's actively learning (recently_learning)
- Include org_teams so coach understands the org chart fiction vs reality
- last_active helps spot disengaged people

## Implementation notes
- Query via CoachContext::getTeam() or getTeams() for multi-team
- Eager load members.skills to avoid N+1
- 'recently_learning' = skills added at Low level in last 60 days
- 'top_skills' = highest level skills, max 3-4

## Pattern reference
Follow existing tool structure in `app/Services/SkillsCoach/Tools/GetUserProfile.php`
close_reason: GetTeamOverview tool is already implemented in app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/GetTeamOverview.php - includes person-centric data with top skills, recently learning, and activity summary.

title: Create FindBackupFor tool
description:
# FindBackupFor Tool

## Purpose
Answer the critical question: 'If X is away, who can cover?'

This is the 'Jim is going on paternity leave - what do we do?!' tool.

## File
`app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/FindBackupFor.php`

## Acceptance criteria - VERIFIED
- [x] Returns coverage breakdown for person query (lines 112-122)
- [x] Returns people list for skill query (lines 170-175)
- [x] Fuzzy matching returns suggestions when no exact skill match (lines 129-137)
- [x] Fuzzy matching returns suggestions when no exact person match (lines 49-57)
- [x] Identifies 'no backup' risks for unique skills (lines 97-99)
- [x] Uses CoachContext::getTeam() to scope query (line 25)
close_reason: All acceptance criteria verified against implementation

title: Create FindMentoringPairs tool
description:
# FindMentoringPairs Tool

## Purpose
The heart of the coach - connecting people to people. Find who could teach whom.

## File
`app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/FindMentoringPairs.php`

## Acceptance Criteria - VERIFIED
- [x] Returns mentor/mentee pairs for skill query (lines 109-114)
- [x] Returns mentors for mentee_name query (lines 162-166)
- [x] Returns mentees for mentor_name query (lines 213-217)
- [x] Returns high-value opportunities when no params given (lines 251-257)
- [x] Fuzzy matching returns suggestions when no exact skill match (lines 55-64)
- [x] Fuzzy matching returns suggestions when no exact person match (lines 121-129, 173-181)

title: Write TeamCoach component tests
Write team tools tests
Refactor Team: replace pivot role with manager_id
description:
## Background

The initial implementation (skillsdb-3tz.1 through 3tz.3) used a pivot table with a 'role' column to track team membership and management. This was based on the original spec which assumed teams could have multiple managers.

After implementation, clarification from the product owner revealed that **teams only ever have one manager** in real-world usage. The pivot role approach is therefore over-engineered.

## Decision

Simplify to use a direct `manager_id` foreign key on the `teams` table instead.

## Changes required

1. Add `manager_id` foreign key to `teams` table migration
2. Remove `role` column from `team_user` pivot table
3. Update Team model:
   - Remove `managers()` relationship
   - Add `manager()` belongsTo relationship
4. Update User model:
   - Simplify `managedTeams()` to use hasMany
   - Update `isTeamManager()` helper
5. Update TestDataSeeder to use new structure
6. Update tests

## Notes

This is a lesson in PM/developer communication - the spec was followed correctly, but the real-world requirement was simpler. Keeping original issues closed to preserve decision history.
close_reason: Refactored to simpler design: manager_id on teams table instead of role column on pivot. Migration, models, seeder, and tests all updated. 256 tests passing.

title: Create TeamSystemPrompt
description:
Create manager-focused system prompt for team coach mode.

## File to create
`app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamSystemPrompt.php`

## Class structure
```php
<?php

namespace App\Services\SkillsCoach;

use App\Models\Team;
use App\Models\User;

class TeamSystemPrompt
{
    public function build(User $user, Team $team): string
    {
        return implode("\n\n", [
            $this->personality(),
            $this->teamContext($team),
            $this->toolGuidance(),
        ]);
    }
    
    // ... private methods
}
```

## Personality section (key excerpts)
```
You are the Team Skills Coach for a university IT team manager.

Your role: Help managers understand their team's capabilities and identify mentoring opportunities that connect people.

What they care about:
- Coverage gaps (single points of failure)
- Development opportunities for team members
- Who could mentor whom
- Where to invest in training

Approach:
- Human-centric: "Alice could mentor Bob on Docker" > "enrol in Docker course"
- Strategic: prioritise recommendations (managers have limited time)
- Respect privacy: don't share individual concerns in hurtful ways
- British sensibility: understated, practical, no corporate cheerleading

Success: You help a manager set up a mentoring relationship that works.
Failure: You just produce generic reports.
```

## Team context section
Include: team name, member count, skill distribution, recent activity, any gaps.

## Pattern reference
Follow structure of `app/Services/SkillsCoach/SystemPrompt.php`.
close_reason: Created TeamSystemPrompt with manager-focused personality and team context

title: Skills Coach (LLM-powered)
description: LLM-powered Skills Coach - but not a generic career chatbot. The real purpose is rebuilding team connection after the amalgamation fragmented the old school-based teams. The coach knows the team history, knows who knows what, and its primary job is connecting humans to humans for mentorship and knowledge sharing. Career advice is the shiny button that draws people in; what they actually get is introductions to experienced colleagues. Tone: warm, collegial, not corporate. Courses are a fallback - real growth comes from talking to people who've fought the same battles. See SKILLS_COACH.md for full context and vision.

title: Verify team tools against acceptance criteria
description:
# Process Failure Remediation

## What happened
The team tools were implemented and closed WITHOUT reading the updated issue specs or verifying against acceptance criteria. The implementer even commented that unchecked boxes were 'a bit odd' - failing to recognise they closed the issues without verification.

## Required steps
1. Reopen tool issues (skillsdb-3tz.13 through 3tz.17)
2. Read each full spec with bd show
3. Compare implementation against acceptance criteria
4. Fix any gaps
5. Check off each criterion as verified
6. Only THEN close the issue

## Lesson
The process exists for a reason. Actually follow the defined workflow.
close_reason: All 5 team tools verified against acceptance criteria. Coach_contactable enhancement tracked in skillsdb-ms9.

What we need is a workflow where enrolling on non-internal courses sends a notification to their team manager.  We need the manager to have a page that shows them the pending enrollments for their team members with a simple approve/reject button which in turn triggers a notification to the person who asked for the enrollment.  We can just use simple laravel queued markdown Mailables for the notifications.

Currently the skills matrix calculates everything on-the-fly, including historical lookups via getSkillLevelAt() in the blade template.

Consider pre-computing the matrix data periodically (daily?) and storing it. Benefits:
- Removes method calls from blade templates
- Faster page loads (no N×M queries for users × skills)
- Enables more complex calculations without impacting UX
- Historical snapshots become trivial lookups

This is tentative - may not be needed if current performance is acceptable.

title: Render markdown in coach responses

## Problem

The coach API responses often contain markdown formatting (headers, lists, bold, etc.) but they're currently displayed as plain text in both SkillsCoach and TeamCoach components.

- resources/views/livewire/skills-coach.blade.php
- resources/views/livewire/admin/team-coach.blade.php

## Solution Options

1. Use a markdown parsing library (e.g. league/commonmark) and render as HTML with appropriate styling
2. Use a JavaScript markdown renderer on the client side
3. Use Tailwind typography plugin (@tailwindcss/typography) with prose classes

## Notes

Low priority - functionality works, this is a polish item.

title: Add coach_contactable awareness to team tools
description:
# Coach Contactable Handling in Team Tools

## Background
During acceptance criteria verification, we discovered team tools don't respect the `coach_contactable` flag. After discussion, we decided on a nuanced approach that turns a privacy constraint into a relationship-building opportunity.

## The Logic
1. If `coach_contactable = true` → show person normally
2. If `coach_contactable = false` AND they're in a team managed by the current user → show normally (it's your own report)
3. If `coach_contactable = false` AND different manager → add note: 'Consider reaching out to their manager [Name] first'

## Why This Approach
- Respects the person's opt-out preference (no cold calls from strangers)
- Creates manager-to-manager dialogue (cross-team relationship building)
- Breaks down silos by creating official channels between teams
- Fits the SKILLS_COACH.md philosophy: human connection over system-directed contact

## Files to Update
- `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/FindBackupFor.php`
- `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/FindMentoringPairs.php`
- `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/GetMemberSkills.php`
- `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/GetRecentActivity.php`
- `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/SuggestTraining.php`
- `app/Services/SkillsCoach/TeamTools/GetTeamOverview.php`

## Implementation
Consider extracting a shared helper method to avoid duplication:
```php
protected function formatPersonWithContactability(User $person, User $currentManager): array
{
    $entry = ['name' => $person->full_name];
    
    if (!$person->coach_contactable && !$this->isDirectReport($person, $currentManager)) {
        $theirManager = $person->teams->first()?->manager;
        if ($theirManager) {
            $entry['note'] = "Consider reaching out to their manager {->full_name} first";
        }
    }
    
    return $entry;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] People with coach_contactable=true shown normally
- [ ] Direct reports shown normally regardless of flag
- [ ] Non-direct-reports with coach_contactable=false include manager suggestion
- [ ] Works correctly when person is in multiple teams
- [ ] Consistent behaviour across all 6 team tools
close_reason: Implemented coach_contactable awareness in all 6 team tools via HandlesContactability trait. Added tests.

title: Add play/animate button to compact matrix time slider

Manual slider dragging works correctly - cells transition and update as expected. The issue appears to be that the Alpine.js interval updating $wire.timelinePosition doesn't trigger Livewire to re-render the grid, even though the slider thumb moves.

title: Livewire: $wire.set() + $nextTick($wire.send()) causes double submission
description:
## Problem

When using Alpine click handlers that combine `$wire.set()` followed by `$nextTick(() => $wire.send())`, the Livewire method gets called twice - once with the intended value, then again with an empty value after the first call completes.

## Symptoms

- Validation errors appearing after successful form submission
- Method being called twice (visible in logs)
- Second call has empty/reset values

## Root Cause

The two-call pattern:
```blade
@click="$wire.set('prompt', suggestion); $nextTick(() => $wire.send())"
```

Creates a race condition or re-trigger after the component re-renders following the first successful call.

## Solution

Either:
1. **Just set the value** - let user submit via form (better UX anyway):
   ```blade
   @click="$wire.set('prompt', suggestion)"
   ```

2. **Single method call** - create a dedicated method that sets and processes in one request:
   ```blade
   @click="$wire.sendSuggestion(suggestion)"
   ```

## Affected Components

- SkillsCoach (personal coach)
- TeamCoach (team coach)

Both fixed by switching to option 1 - populating the composer and letting user submit.
close_reason: Fixed by changing suggestion buttons to just set the prompt value, letting users submit via the form. Simpler and better UX.
